gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The semantic-token **type legend**, in index order. The encoded stream's `tokenType` field is an"] # [doc = " index into this list (LSP §`semanticTokens`). These are the standard LSP token types the ratified"] # [doc = " RFC-0026 §3.2 table maps each lexer bucket to (the TextMate/tree-sitter names of the same table"] # [doc = " live in `tools/grammar/`), **plus `string`** (index 8, M-975): RFC-0026 §3.2's table predates the"] # [doc = " textual-string-literal token ([`Tok::StrLit`], M-910/M-911) and has no bucket for it. `string` is"] # [doc = " still a **standard, unsuffixed** LSP semantic-token type (the same naming convention the ratified"] # [doc = " table already follows), added here **additively** (appended, not renumbering any existing index)"] # [doc = " so this classifier can honestly tag `StrLit` rather than silently dropping it (G2). This mirrors"] # [doc = " the §3.2 coverage note's own precedent: `operator`/`identifier` already ship **LSP-only**, ahead"] # [doc = " of the other layers. FLAG (Declared, not yet ratified): a future RFC-0026 amendment/supersession"] # [doc = " should fold `string` into the normative table so the TextMate/tree-sitter layers can pick it up too."] pub const TOKEN_TYPES : & [& str] = & ["keyword" , "type" , "enumMember" , "function" , "variable" , "number" , "operator" , "comment" , "string" ,] ;
gaps.reason: top-level `const TOKEN_TYPES` — co-emit (ORACLE-R1 A4) only covers plain unsigned integer types (`u8`/`u16`/`u32`/`u64`/`u128`/`usize`); other types have no const item production and no faithful zero-arg-fn encoding (gap, never fabricate)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The `legend` advertised in the server's `semanticTokensProvider` capability: the type list above"] # [doc = " and an empty modifier list (no modifiers are emitted — honest about scope)."] # [must_use] pub fn semantic_tokens_legend () -> Value { json ! ({ "tokenTypes" : TOKEN_TYPES , "tokenModifiers" : [] , }) }
gaps.reason: expression-position macro `json` — no macro system in this grammar fragment (`write!`/`format!` lower to pure `Bytes` per DN-127/M-1090 WU-3)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Classify one lexical item to its [`TOKEN_TYPES`] index, or `None` for items that carry no"] # [doc = " highlight (delimiters, `Eof`). Delimiters (`()[]{}`, `:` `,` `.`) are intentionally unclassified:"] # [doc = " editors colour them via the grammar, not semantic tokens. `<`/`>` are **not** in that set — since"] # [doc = " RFC-0037 D1 moved type-argument lists to `[…]`, [`Tok::LAngle`]/[`Tok::RAngle`] are pure infix"] # [doc = " comparison operators (`lt`/`gt`), so they classify with the other operators below (never silently"] # [doc = " dropped, G2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Never-silent completeness (M-975):** every [`Tok`] variant is either matched below or falls into"] # [doc = " the documented delimiter/`Eof` `None` arm — there is no wildcard catch-all arm, so the match is"] # [doc = " exhaustive over every [`Tok`] variant the compiler knows about: a future lexer token that isn't"] # [doc = " added here fails to **compile**, rather than silently falling through unhighlighted (G2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `pub(crate)` (not public API) so the in-crate test module (`src/tests/semantic.rs`) can exercise"] # [doc = " it directly."] pub (crate) fn classify (kind : & LexKind) -> Option < u32 > { let tok = match kind { LexKind :: Comment => return Some (T_COMMENT) , LexKind :: Token (t) => t , } ; let idx = match tok { Tok :: Nodule | Tok :: Phylum | Tok :: Colony | Tok :: Hypha | Tok :: Fuse | Tok :: Mesh | Tok :: Graft | Tok :: Cyst | Tok :: Xloc | Tok :: Forage | Tok :: Backbone | Tok :: Tier | Tok :: Reclaim | Tok :: Consume | Tok :: Grow | Tok :: Derive | Tok :: Use | Tok :: Pub | Tok :: Priv | Tok :: Type | Tok :: Trait | Tok :: Impl | Tok :: Fn | Tok :: Matured | Tok :: Thaw | Tok :: Let | Tok :: In | Tok :: If | Tok :: Then | Tok :: Else | Tok :: Match | Tok :: For | Tok :: Swap | Tok :: Default | Tok :: Paradigm | Tok :: With | Tok :: Wild | Tok :: Spore | Tok :: Wrapping | Tok :: To | Tok :: Policy | Tok :: Lambda | Tok :: Object | Tok :: Via | Tok :: Lower => T_KEYWORD , Tok :: Binary | Tok :: Ternary | Tok :: Dense | Tok :: Vsa | Tok :: BinShort | Tok :: TernShort | Tok :: EmbShort | Tok :: HvecShort | Tok :: Seq | Tok :: Bytes | Tok :: Float | Tok :: Substrate | Tok :: Sparse | Tok :: Scalar (_) => T_TYPE , Tok :: Strength (_) => T_ENUM_MEMBER , Tok :: Ident (_) => T_VARIABLE , Tok :: BinLit (_) | Tok :: TritLit (_) | Tok :: Int (_) | Tok :: BytesLit (_) | Tok :: FloatLit (_) => { T_NUMBER } Tok :: StrLit (_) => T_STRING , Tok :: Plus | Tok :: Minus | Tok :: Star | Tok :: Slash | Tok :: Percent | Tok :: Question | Tok :: Caret | Tok :: Amp | Tok :: AmpAmp | Tok :: Eq | Tok :: EqEq | Tok :: Arrow | Tok :: FatArrow | Tok :: Bang | Tok :: BangEq | Tok :: Pipe | Tok :: PipePipe | Tok :: At | Tok :: AtStdSys | Tok :: LAngle | Tok :: RAngle | Tok :: Shl | Tok :: Shr => T_OPERATOR , Tok :: LParen | Tok :: RParen | Tok :: LBrace | Tok :: RBrace | Tok :: LBracket | Tok :: RBracket | Tok :: Colon | Tok :: ColonColon | Tok :: Comma | Tok :: Semi | Tok :: Dot | Tok :: Eof => return None , } ; Some (idx) }

# [doc = " Build the `textDocument/semanticTokens/full` result for `src`: the LSP relative-delta encoding"] # [doc = " (`{ \"data\": [deltaLine, deltaStartChar, length, tokenType, tokenModifiers, …] }`)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The encoding is relative to the previous emitted token (LSP §`semanticTokens` \"Integer Encoding\"):"] # [doc = " `deltaLine` is the line gap; `deltaStartChar` is the column gap when on the same line, else the"] # [doc = " absolute 0-based start column; `length`/`tokenType` are the item's char length and legend index;"] # [doc = " `tokenModifiers` is always `0`. Items are emitted in source order (already sorted by line/col)."] # [doc = " Unclassified items (delimiters) are skipped. **Never-silent:** an un-lexable document yields"] # [doc = " `{ \"data\": [] }` (the diagnostics channel reports the error), never a fabricated stream (G2)."] # [must_use] pub fn semantic_tokens_full (src : & str) -> Value { json ! ({ "data" : encode (& lex_items (src)) }) }
gaps.reason: expression-position macro `json` — no macro system in this grammar fragment (`write!`/`format!` lower to pure `Bytes` per DN-127/M-1090 WU-3)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The flat `u32` delta-stream for `items` (the body of [`semantic_tokens_full`], exposed for tests)."] # [must_use] pub (crate) fn encode (items : & [LexItem]) -> Vec < u32 > { let mut data = Vec :: new () ; let mut prev_line = 0u32 ; let mut prev_col0 = 0u32 ; for it in items { let Some (ttype) = classify (& it . kind) else { continue ; } ; let line0 = it . line . saturating_sub (1) ; let col0 = it . col . saturating_sub (1) ; let delta_line = line0 - prev_line ; let delta_col = if delta_line == 0 { col0 - prev_col0 } else { col0 } ; data . extend_from_slice (& [delta_line , delta_col , it . len , ttype , 0]) ; prev_line = line0 ; prev_col0 = col0 ; } data }
gaps.reason: qualified/associated-function call `Vec :: new` did not resolve to a known-emitted associated fn — Mycelium calls are bare identifiers, and the only sound surface form for `Type::method(...)` is the mangled `Type__method` name the declaration side already emits for a receiver-less inherent-impl associated fn (DN-34 §8.13/8.14), referenced only when that exact declaration is PROVEN present (same-file, or a resolved M-1084 cross-nodule sibling). A primitive/std associated fn with no emitted decl (e.g. `i128::try_from`), an unresolved type, or a `self`-receiving method (excluded by construction — it stays reachable only from its own bare call site) all gap here rather than fabricate a call (G2/VR-5, DN-133)
